pair_convention.spot_explanation: EUR/JPY at 183.63 means 1 EUR buys 183.63 JPY. A rising pair indicates EUR is strengthening against JPY; a falling pair indicates EUR is weakening against JPY (or equivalently, JPY is strengthening against EUR). The pair sits at the 90th percentile of its 52-week range (154.80–186.88), near its all-time high of 186.88 set on 23 January 2026.
pair_convention.bearish_means: pair FALLS (EUR weakens vs JPY / JPY strengthens vs EUR)

executive_summary: EUR/JPY trades at 183.63, meaning 1 euro buys 183.63 yen, with the pair sitting in the 90th percentile of its 52-week range after rallying approximately 9.7% over the past year from the February 2025 low of 154.80 to the January 2026 all-time high of 186.88. Performance has been essentially flat over 1 month (+0.04%) and 3 months (-0.03%), signalling a loss of upside momentum near the cycle highs. The dominant bilateral narrative is a collision between two currencies suffering distinct but overlapping vulnerabilities to the Iran-US war: the eurozone faces an emerging stagflation shock from imported energy costs while Japan confronts catastrophic terms-of-trade deterioration as a 90% energy importer. The critical bilateral divergence from standalone dossier views is that both the EUR and JPY dossiers are directionally aligned toward weakness against the USD — but against each other, the BoJ's active normalisation cycle and MoF intervention risk at USD/JPY 160 provide JPY with a unique catalyst that EUR lacks. FXStreet's Q2 2026 outlook identifies EUR/JPY as 'one of the strongest expressions,' citing the macro split between ECB growth fragility and BoJ tightening bias. Our headline stance is Bearish EUR/JPY (pair falling), conviction 3/5 tactically and 3/5 strategically, reflecting the BoJ's policy advantage as the single most important bilateral differentiator. The tactical bearish case is dominated by the rate differential narrowing dynamic and bilateral positioning unwind risk, while the strategic case rests on the BoJ's multi-step normalisation cycle progressively compressing the ECB-BoJ spread from +125bp toward +50-100bp over the coming quarters.

market_context.valuation_assessment: EUR/JPY at 183.63 trades approximately 5% above the PPP-implied fair value of approximately 175 (from the EUR dossier) or ~104 from the JPY dossier's bilateral calculation — the wide discrepancy reflects different PPP methodologies. Using the EUR dossier's -5% deviation versus JPY and the JPY dossier's -43% deviation versus EUR, the pair is trading significantly above longer-term equilibrium. The BIS REER for JPY sits at its all-time low of 52.9 (2005=100), while EUR's REER at 103.85 is near its long-run average — suggesting the valuation asymmetry is concentrated on JPY's extreme cheapness rather than EUR's expensiveness.

positioning.net_pair_implication: EUR asset managers at the 95th percentile long and JPY specs at the 32nd percentile short are both directionally aligned toward a higher EUR/JPY. However, the massive EUR AM long represents a liquidation overhang while the JPY short is moderate and actively covering (+10,577 latest week). A simultaneous unwind of both legs — EUR longs liquidating and JPY shorts covering — would produce a sharp EUR/JPY decline far greater than either leg alone.
positioning.crowding_assessment: Pair trade crowded on long side — EUR leg at extreme, JPY short leg moderate

pair_technicals.key_moving_averages: 50d SMA: 183.20 (spot marginally above); 100d SMA: 183.64 (spot essentially flat against); 200d SMA: 180.34 (spot well above by 329 pips). Configuration is neutral-to-bullish with spot oscillating around the 50d and 100d averages rather than trending cleanly.
pair_technicals.chart_pattern: Pair compressing in a narrowing range between 182.00 and 185.50 since mid-February 2026, having corrected from the 186.88 all-time high. Potential double-top structure forming at the 186 area; RSI divergence and MACD bearish crossover noted on daily timeframe. ActionForex identifies a correction from 186.86 that should have completed at 180.78, with a break above 184.75 needed to retest the 186.22/86 resistance zone.

pair_volatility.rv_vs_iv: Realised volatility has been suppressed in recent weeks with 20-day daily ranges averaging approximately 100-130 pips, broadly consistent with implied vol. The risk premium on options is modest, suggesting options are approximately fairly priced on a trailing basis.
pair_volatility.risk_reversals: EUR/JPY risk reversals not directly available; inferred from component pairs (EUR puts bid on EUR/USD, JPY puts bid on USD/JPY) suggests EUR/JPY puts carry a modest premium, consistent with the directional bearish skew on the cross.
pair_volatility.vol_term_structure: Vol term structure should exhibit backwardation around the late-April BoJ/ECB/FOMC cluster. The density of event risk in the April 27-30 window (BoJ MPM + ECB decision) should create a vol premium for 1M over 3M, with the curve flattening thereafter.

market_context.recent_regime: EUR/JPY has been trading in an increasingly compressed range between approximately 182.00 and 185.50 since mid-February 2026, having corrected from the 186.88 all-time high. The dominant narrative is one of offsetting forces: the Iran-driven energy shock is EUR-negative (stagflation) and JPY-negative (terms of trade), creating a tug-of-war that has produced range-bound price action. The pair briefly tested 181.19 on 11 February before recovering, establishing a clear demand zone. The lack of directional conviction reflects the market's struggle to determine which currency suffers more from the energy shock — a question that the BoJ's April 27-28 meeting may decisively answer.

bilateral_carry_analysis.breakeven_move: Spot can move approximately 1.3% against position before carry is erased over 3M (45-50 pips carry over 4 weeks vs 80-130 pip daily range)

forward_points.1m: -10 pips (EUR at modest premium, reflecting positive carry)

bilateral_carry_analysis.carry_assessment: The ECB deposit rate at 2.00% versus the BoJ rate at 0.75% produces a +125bp annualised carry in favour of EUR/JPY longs. Over a 4-week tactical horizon (20 trading days), this carry equates to approximately 45-50 pips, which is modest relative to the pair's current daily range of 80-130 pips. If the BoJ hikes to 1.00% in April, the carry narrows to +100bp, further reducing the incentive for carry-seeking longs. The carry-to-vol ratio is approximately 0.09-0.13 (125bp carry / ~10-12% implied vol), well below the 0.40+ threshold that historically attracts aggressive carry accumulation. The carry is positive but insufficient to compensate for directional risk in the current environment — a single BoJ hike would move the pair more in one session than the carry earns in a quarter. The carry cost of being short EUR/JPY is minimal (~60 pips over 6 months), making the position cheap to hold from the funding perspective.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: The ECB-BoJ rate differential at +125bp is the narrowest since the BoJ began normalising in October 2024, and is poised to narrow further to +100bp if the BoJ delivers a 25bp hike to 1.00% on April 27-28 (70% OIS probability). This is the defining bilateral dynamic: the BoJ is the only G10 central bank where the next move is a rate hike at a specific, imminent meeting, while the ECB is in a holding pattern with ambiguous guidance (40-50% hike probability at April 30 meeting, but without new staff projections). The front-end repricing is asymmetric — JGB 2Y at 1.32% has been rising rapidly on normalisation expectations while Bund 2Y at 2.65% surged on inflation fear rather than growth optimism, a distinction that matters for FX. The Bund-JGB 10Y spread has narrowed to approximately 70bp from wider levels, reflecting JGB yields rising faster than Bund yields. The BoJ's Summary of Opinions (March 30) revealed debate not just about timing but about the size of the next hike — a 50bp hike is essentially unpriced and would be explosively bearish for EUR/JPY.
key_bilateral_metrics: ECB-BoJ policy rate differential: +125bp (ECB 2.00% vs BoJ 0.75%), narrowing to +100bp on BoJ April hike
Bund-JGB 10Y spread: ~70bp (narrowing; JGB yields rising faster)
OIS-implied BoJ hike April 27-28: ~70% for +25bp; near-zero for 50bp
ECB April 30 hike probability: ~40-50% (no new projections, making action unlikely)
Annualised carry: +125bp (shrinking)
tactical.what_is_priced: The market is pricing a ~70% probability of an April BoJ hike, which is broadly fair. What is underpriced is the tail risk of a 50bp BoJ move and the momentum of the normalisation cycle beyond April — the Summary of Opinions makes clear this is actively debated. The ECB's uncertain path is priced as a coin flip.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Over 1-6 months, the rate differential trajectory is clearly EUR/JPY-bearish. The BoJ is projected to deliver 1-2 additional hikes to 1.00-1.25%, while the ECB faces a bimodal distribution (0-2 hikes). In the most likely scenario — BoJ hikes twice while the ECB hikes once — the differential compresses from +125bp to approximately +50-75bp. In the extreme scenario where the BoJ reaches 1.25% and the ECB stays at 2.00%, the spread narrows to +75bp. If the ECB hikes to 2.50%, the spread could actually widen to +125-150bp — but this scenario requires the energy shock to persist, which would also damage EUR through other channels. The carry dynamic is shifting from EUR-positive to increasingly neutral as the BoJ normalises, removing one of the fundamental pillars supporting EUR/JPY at elevated levels. The JGB 30Y at 3.71% reflects a structural repricing of Japanese government bond risk that is fundamentally altering the Japanese fixed income landscape and gradually pulling capital home.
key_bilateral_metrics: Projected BoJ rate by Q3 2026: 1.00-1.25% (from 0.75%)
Projected ECB rate by Q3 2026: 2.00-2.50% (uncertainty wide)
Projected carry narrowing: 125bp → 50-100bp
JGB 30Y: 3.71% (+117bp YoY) — attracting domestic capital
BoJ terminal rate range: 1.00% (Goldman) to 1.75% (Tamura)
strategic.what_is_priced: Consensus forecasts for EUR/JPY suggest a gradual decline toward the low 180s by year-end. The market underprices the possibility that BoJ normalisation triggers a carry trade unwind — an estimated $500bn in outstanding JPY carry positions could compress the pair far more rapidly than the gradual consensus decline implies.

factors.horizon_split: Tactical confidence is higher (70 vs 60) because the April BoJ meeting is a specific, datable catalyst. Strategically, the normalisation pace beyond April is less certain, and the ECB's own potential hikes could partially offset the BoJ's narrowing.

carry_analysis.summary: The +125bp carry favour EUR/JPY longs but is insufficient to compensate for directional risk. Carry-to-vol ratio of ~0.11 is well below the 0.40+ threshold for aggressive carry accumulation. A BoJ hike to 1.00% narrows carry to +100bp. The 6M carry cost of being short EUR/JPY is only ~60 pips — making the bearish expression cheap to hold. Forward points are modest (-10/-30/-60 for 1M/3M/6M), confirming carry does not create meaningful headwind for shorts.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: The bilateral growth-inflation picture clearly favours JPY over EUR. The eurozone faces a stagflationary setup — the worst data flow configuration for a currency — with Composite PMI at 50.5 (near stagnation), ZEW collapsing 58.8 points, and headline HICP spiking toward 2.5-3.0%. Japan's economy is in better shape: GDP at 1.3% annualised well above the 0.5% potential rate, unemployment at 2.6% (tight), and Shunto wage results at 5.26% validating the wage-price mechanism. Japan's headline CPI softness (1.3%) is misleading — driven by government energy subsidies — while core-core CPI at 2.5% remains above target. The eurozone's data surprise index is deteriorating (IFO, ZEW, PMI all declining) while Japan's Tankan and Reuters Tankan suggest continued resilience. The bilateral growth differential has shifted from roughly neutral to EUR-negative, as the energy shock disproportionately impacts eurozone industrial production versus Japan's services-led domestic demand.
key_bilateral_metrics: Eurozone Composite PMI: 50.5 vs Japan Jibun Bank Composite: 52.5
Eurozone GDP QoQ: +0.2% (Q4) vs Japan: +0.3% (Q4)
Eurozone headline HICP: ~2.5-3.0% (March) vs Japan headline CPI: 1.3% (Feb)
ZEW expectations: -0.5 (collapsed) vs Tankan large manufacturers: +15
Eurozone unemployment: 6.1% vs Japan: 2.6%
tactical.what_is_priced: The market has partially priced eurozone growth weakness through EUR/USD depreciation but not through EUR/JPY specifically — the cross has remained flat, suggesting the market treats the energy shock as equally damaging to both currencies. This is incorrect: the eurozone's manufacturing-heavy economy and higher energy import bill relative to GDP make it more vulnerable to the stagflation squeeze.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Strategically, the growth picture becomes more balanced. The German fiscal expansion (€500bn infrastructure fund) is a genuine game-changer for eurozone growth potential, with EC estimates of +0.3-0.5pp GDP impact by 2027. Japan's fiscal impulse under Takaichi (JPY122T record budget) is expansionary but less transformative. If the energy shock proves transitory, the eurozone's cyclical recovery could reassert, supported by fiscal spending. Japan's strategic growth trajectory is positive but constrained by demographics. The inflation picture matters bilaterally: if energy prices normalise, eurozone headline HICP falls back quickly while Japan's core-core inflation persists — supporting continued BoJ normalisation relative to a potentially pausing ECB. The net effect at 6 months is genuinely ambiguous.
key_bilateral_metrics: German fiscal impulse: €500bn over 12 years vs Japan FY2026 budget: JPY122T (record)
EC GDP impact estimate: +0.3-0.5pp by 2027 (eurozone)
ECB 2026 GDP forecast: 0.9% vs BoJ 2026 GDP forecast: ~1.1%
Eurozone services HICP: 3.4% vs Japan core-core CPI: 2.5%
strategic.what_is_priced: The German fiscal expansion is partially priced in European equities and Bund yields but not yet fully reflected in EUR/JPY. If the growth impact materialises in H2 2026, it would be EUR/JPY-supportive — but this is a 6-12 month story, not actionable now.

factors.horizon_split: Tactically bearish EUR/JPY on the near-term data flow asymmetry (eurozone stagflation vs Japan resilience). Strategically neutral as German fiscal expansion could offset Japan's demographic constraints.

factors.weight_adjustment_reason: EUR/JPY is a moderate-to-high risk-sensitivity cross; risk-off has outsized pair impact. Weight increased +0.03 on both horizons.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: EUR/JPY is a moderate risk-sensitivity cross — historically it tracks global equity performance and carry-trade appetite. With the VIX at ~30, the S&P 500 down 7-9% from highs, and the Nikkei 225 down 11.5% in a month, the risk environment is hostile for EUR/JPY longs. The pair's risk beta is positive (rising in risk-on, falling in risk-off), meaning the current risk-off episode should weigh on EUR/JPY. However, the correlation has been disrupted by the energy shock: normally JPY would strengthen more in risk-off, but the Iran conflict is suppressing JPY's safe-haven mechanism through the energy import channel. ING's outlook noted that downside risks for EUR/JPY exist if global equities correct, as the pair's performance has 'often moved in step with equity-market trends and carry-trade appetite.' The Nikkei's 11.5% decline could trigger Japanese institutional repatriation if losses compound with unhedged currency losses — a direct EUR/JPY-negative flow.
key_bilateral_metrics: VIX: ~30 (90th percentile); Nikkei 225: -11.5% from highs
EUR/JPY historical beta to risk: moderately positive (~0.3-0.4)
AUD/JPY (primary risk barometer): under stress at ~109
Euro Stoxx 50: -10.6% from ATH; short interest +31.8%
tactical.what_is_priced: The market has priced moderate risk-off but not extreme stress. If the 6 April Iran deadline triggers escalation, VIX could spike above 40 and equity drawdowns deepen — this would be EUR/JPY-negative as carry unwinds accelerate.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Over 1-6 months, the risk picture is a wash for EUR/JPY. If the Iran conflict resolves, risk appetite recovers, which is traditionally EUR/JPY-positive. If it escalates, risk-off deepens, which is EUR/JPY-negative. The global hawkish monetary pivot creates tighter financial conditions that historically support JPY — suggesting the strategic risk environment is mildly EUR/JPY-bearish, but the magnitude depends on outcomes impossible to forecast.
key_bilateral_metrics: Global monetary tightening: RBA, ECB, BoE all repricing hawkishly
Historical post-crisis EUR/JPY pattern: sharp rallies on risk recovery
Carry trade unwind risk: elevated if vol sustains above 12%
strategic.what_is_priced: Markets price a contained crisis. Any resolution pathway triggers a EUR/JPY rally on carry re-engagement; any escalation triggers EUR/JPY decline on equity correlation.

factors.horizon_split: Tactically bearish on active risk-off and Nikkei weakness. Strategically neutral as the outcome depends on unresolvable geopolitical variables.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: The positioning picture is the most compelling bilateral signal for EUR/JPY bears. EUR asset managers hold +287,940 net long contracts (95th percentile, ~€36bn notional) — a massive overhang built during the German fiscal expansion rally. JPY specs are net short -62,806 contracts (32nd percentile) — moderate and actively covering (+10,577 latest week). For EUR/JPY, these positions are directionally aligned: long EUR and short JPY both support a higher cross. But the asymmetry of unwind risk is bearish: the EUR AM long is near multi-year extremes and vulnerable to liquidation if the energy-driven stagflation narrative intensifies, while the JPY short is moderate and would accelerate covering if the BoJ hikes or MoF intervenes. A simultaneous unwind of both legs — EUR longs liquidating, JPY shorts covering — would produce a sharp EUR/JPY decline far greater than either leg alone. The leveraged fund covering in JPY (+10,577 in latest week) is a leading indicator that fast money is reducing JPY-bearish exposure ahead of the April catalyst cluster.
key_bilateral_metrics: EUR AM net long: +287,940 (95th percentile) — massive liquidation risk
JPY net short: -62,806 (32nd percentile) — moderate, actively covering
Combined bilateral signal: both legs aligned for EUR/JPY downside on unwind
EUR AM notional: ~€36bn; JPY short notional: ~$6.9bn
MoF intervention as exogenous squeeze catalyst for JPY leg
tactical.what_is_priced: The market has not priced the bilateral positioning unwind scenario. A combined EUR long liquidation + JPY short covering triggered by a BoJ hike and energy shock escalation could compress EUR/JPY by 300-500 pips in 1-2 weeks — an event risk that positioning amplifies rather than creates.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Over 1-6 months, the positioning overhang either liquidates (removing the risk but also the signal) or validates (if the structural EUR bullish thesis plays out). The structural institutional flow picture is mixed: Japanese life insurers at 45.7% hedge ratio (13-year low) represent persistent JPY selling through foreign asset holdings, while the IMF COFER data shows EUR's reserve share gradually rising to 20.33%. Neither creates a strong directional signal at the 6-month horizon. The $500bn JPY carry trade can persist as long as differentials remain positive and vol stays contained.
key_bilateral_metrics: EUR reserve share: 20.33% (slowly rising)
Japanese life insurer hedge ratio: 45.7% (structural JPY selling)
JPY carry trade outstanding: ~$500bn (Morgan Stanley)
EU-Japan bilateral FDI: €212.5bn Japanese investment in EU vs €86.6bn EU investment in Japan
strategic.what_is_priced: Structural flows are not priced in the traditional sense. The net positioning signal is neutral strategically — the near-term liquidation risk will have resolved by then.

factors.horizon_split: Tactically bearish (65) on the powerful bilateral positioning unwind catalyst. Strategically neutral (50) as positioning resets over 1-6 months.

tactical.combined_positioning_signal: Long EUR (95th percentile AM) + short JPY (32nd percentile) = both legs directionally aligned for EUR/JPY upside — but the extreme EUR long is the crowded consensus trade most vulnerable to reversal
tactical.unwind_trigger: BoJ hike on April 27-28 triggering JPY short covering, simultaneously with EUR AM liquidation on stagflation narrative intensification
tactical.historical_analogue: August 2024: EUR/JPY fell ~800 pips in days as carry trades unwound simultaneously across multiple legs when BoJ hiked unexpectedly
tactical.contrarian_case: The contrarian case is EUR/JPY sharply lower: the EUR AM long at the 95th percentile is one of the most extreme positioning readings in the G10 complex. If energy shock stagflation narrative dominates and the BoJ delivers its expected hike, both positioning legs unwind simultaneously — long EUR liquidation plus JPY short covering — creating a compressed move of 300-500 pips in 1-2 weeks that the consensus gradual-decline scenario completely underestimates. Fast money has already started reducing JPY shorts (+10,577 latest week), which is the canary in the coal mine.

strategic.combined_positioning_signal: Over 1-6 months, the extreme EUR AM long will either have liquidated (bearish resolution) or been validated (bullish resolution). The JPY short at 32nd percentile can rebuild or continue unwinding. Net: no extreme positioning signal persists at strategic horizon.
strategic.unwind_trigger: Failure of German fiscal expansion narrative to materialise in data, combined with BoJ delivering 2+ hikes
strategic.historical_analogue: 2015-2016: EUR/JPY declined from 140 to 110 over 18 months as BoJ QE expansion boosted JPY while ECB easing capped EUR — the reverse carry compression dynamic
strategic.contrarian_case: The strategic contrarian case is that the consensus EUR/JPY decline scenario of 182-185 by year-end is too modest. If the BoJ normalises aggressively to 1.25%+ and the EUR AM long (currently at 95th percentile) fully liquidates, EUR/JPY could compress to 168-173 — a scenario that none of the major bank forecasts currently entertain. The $500bn carry trade overhang represents a potential cascade that could make the 2024 unwind look modest by comparison.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: The terms-of-trade shock creates an unusual bilateral dynamic: both the eurozone and Japan are net energy importers hit by the Iran-driven oil and gas price spike, but through different channels and magnitudes. Japan imports ~90% of its energy and faces a catastrophic ToT deterioration (Brent +55%, JKM LNG +57%). The eurozone is also a net energy importer (~58% dependency) with TTF gas up ~70%. Using the framework's commodity direction rules, oil up is JPY strongly negative and EUR moderately negative. Net effect for EUR/JPY: mildly bearish, as the magnitude of JPY's energy sensitivity exceeds EUR's — but only mildly because both currencies suffer. The eurozone current account surplus (€255bn, 1.6% GDP, declining) versus Japan's surplus (driven by primary income at JPY41.59T record, but goods balance deteriorating) creates roughly offsetting flows. Japanese investment stock in the EU is very significant at €212.5 billion, concentrated in the Netherlands and Germany. This bilateral investment flow is structurally EUR/JPY-supportive, but most income is reinvested rather than repatriated.
key_bilateral_metrics: Eurozone net energy import dependency: ~58% vs Japan: ~90%
Brent impact: EUR moderately negative vs JPY strongly negative
TTF gas: EUR negative; JKM LNG: JPY strongly negative
Eurozone C/A: €255bn surplus (declining) vs Japan C/A: surplus (primary income driven)
EU-Japan bilateral goods trade: ~€142bn combined (2024-2025)
tactical.what_is_priced: The market is pricing both currencies as energy-shock losers, which is why EUR/JPY has been flat. The marginal difference in energy sensitivity (Japan more exposed on oil, Europe more exposed on gas) roughly cancels out. No mispricing evident on this factor.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Strategically, the external balance channel is a wash for EUR/JPY. If oil normalises, both currencies benefit — Japan perhaps more than the eurozone because Japan's goods trade deficit is more energy-price-sensitive. If oil stays elevated, both suffer, though again Japan slightly more. The eurozone's structural advantage is the German fiscal expansion creating investment inflows, while Japan's advantage is the massive primary income surplus (JPY41.59T). The IMF's 2026 Article IV projects Japan's current account surplus to 'remain strong in 2026, driven primarily by an income balance surplus earned from Japan's large stock of net foreign assets.' The net bilateral effect is genuinely neutral — the energy shock's asymmetric impact is offset by structural flow differences.
key_bilateral_metrics: Oil normalisation scenario: Japan benefits more (larger ToT improvement)
Gas normalisation: Eurozone benefits more (TTF directly affects EU)
Japan primary income: JPY41.59T (record) vs EU services surplus with Japan: €17.6bn
US tariff exposure: both face Section 301/122; Japan more exposed on autos
strategic.what_is_priced: No material mispricing. Both currencies face similar, though not identical, external balance headwinds.

factors.horizon_split: Both horizons neutral. The energy shock's bilateral impact is roughly symmetric, with Japan slightly more vulnerable on oil but the eurozone more vulnerable on gas.

net_commodity_exposure.base_commodity_profile: EUR — net energy importer (~58% dependency); TTF gas highly relevant; moderately negative on oil/gas price spikes; no significant commodity exports
net_commodity_exposure.quote_commodity_profile: JPY — net energy importer (~90% dependency); heavily exposed to Brent crude and JKM LNG; strongly negative on energy price spikes; no commodity export offset
net_commodity_exposure.net_effect_calculation: Step 1: Oil +55% (Brent) — EUR moderately negative (−); JPY strongly negative (−−). Net EUR/JPY effect: mildly bearish (JPY hurt more). Step 2: Gas — TTF +70% hits EUR directly (−); JKM LNG +57% hits JPY directly (−−). Net EUR/JPY effect: mildly bearish (Japan's total energy import ratio higher). Step 3: Combine oil and gas effects — both currencies are net losers, but Japan's 90% energy import dependency versus eurozone's 58% means Japan suffers marginally more from the combined shock. Net bilateral: approximately neutral with a mild JPY-negative tilt, translating to a mild EUR/JPY bullish effect from the ToT channel alone — but this is overwhelmed by other factors. The two effects partially offset: eurozone more exposed to gas (TTF), Japan more exposed to oil (Brent/JKM), resulting in a roughly symmetric impact.
net_commodity_exposure.scenario_oil_up_10pct: Mild EUR/JPY-bullish: JPY hurt more than EUR from oil given 90% vs 58% import dependency; net pair moves approximately +50-100 pips in the near term
net_commodity_exposure.scenario_oil_down_10pct: Mild EUR/JPY-bearish: JPY benefits more than EUR from energy cost relief; JPY's terms-of-trade improvement is proportionally larger; net pair moves approximately -50-100 pips
net_commodity_exposure.dominant_commodity_driver: Brent crude oil is the dominant commodity driver for this pair. Japan's 90% energy import dependency makes oil the single largest bilateral terms-of-trade differentiator; TTF gas matters for EUR but is secondary to the oil channel for EUR/JPY direction.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: The guidance divergence is the second most important bilateral signal after the rate differential itself. The BoJ's communication is unambiguously hawkish: Takata dissented for an immediate hike, the Summary of Opinions revealed debate about the size of the next move, and Ueda explicitly kept the April hike on the table. The BoJ has built a track record of delivery (three hikes as signalled). The ECB's communication is ambiguous: Lagarde left the door open to 'measured adjustment' but Villeroy called it 'premature' to specify timing. The Governing Council is split — Nagel hawkish, Lane measured, Villeroy cautious. This dispersion introduces policy uncertainty that markets penalise. The net bilateral signal: BoJ guidance is clear, directional, and credible (April hike highly probable); ECB guidance is ambiguous, dispersed, and uncertain (April action unlikely without new projections). This asymmetry directly supports a lower EUR/JPY.
key_bilateral_metrics: BoJ: 8-1 hold (Takata dissented); explicit April hike signal
ECB: unanimous hold; split on forward path (Nagel vs Villeroy)
BoJ delivery track record: 3/3 hikes as signalled
ECB April: no new staff projections = unlikely to hike
Guidance gap: BoJ converging on action; ECB diverging on direction
tactical.what_is_priced: The BoJ's hawkish guidance is ~70% priced for April. The ECB's ambiguity is fully priced (40-50% probability for April, reflecting genuine uncertainty). The bilateral edge is that the BoJ is more likely to deliver a positive surprise (50bp or hawkish forward guidance) than the ECB.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Over 1-6 months, the BoJ's normalisation trajectory remains the stronger guidance signal. The June MPM includes the interim QT assessment, and the July meeting with updated Outlook Report is historically one of the BoJ's most consequential — potentially delivering a second hike. The ECB's June meeting with staff projections is the key strategic event for EUR. If both central banks hike, the bilateral effect is ambiguous. But the direction of travel is clear: the BoJ is moving from 0.75% toward 1.00-1.25% (upward), while the ECB's path is genuinely uncertain (could be 2.00-2.75%). The BoJ's institutional credibility on the normalisation path has never been higher; the ECB's credibility is being tested by the stagflation dilemma.
key_bilateral_metrics: BoJ strategic meetings: April (hike), June (QT assessment), July (Outlook Report + possible 2nd hike)
ECB strategic meetings: April (hold), June (projections + possible 1st hike)
BoJ terminal rate debate: 1.00-1.75% vs ECB terminal: 2.00-2.75%
IMF endorsement of BoJ normalisation vs IMF caution on ECB responding to supply shock
strategic.what_is_priced: The market prices BoJ terminal at ~1.00-1.25% and ECB at ~2.25-2.50% — the gap narrows strategically but remains positive for EUR/JPY carry. The underpriced element is BoJ acceleration risk (Tamura's 1.50-1.75%).

factors.horizon_split: Both horizons bearish EUR/JPY on guidance divergence. Tactical confidence higher (65 vs 55) because the April BoJ meeting is the immediate delivery point.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: The event calendar is extraordinarily dense and symmetric in its potential EUR/JPY impact. The April 6 Iran deadline is the dominant binary event: escalation would push oil higher — marginally more JPY-negative than EUR-negative (EUR/JPY mildly bullish on escalation). De-escalation would collapse oil — more JPY-positive than EUR-positive (EUR/JPY bearish on de-escalation, as JPY safe-haven restoration would outweigh EUR benefit). The BoJ April 27-28 meeting is EUR/JPY-bearish if the hike is delivered. The ECB April 30 meeting is ambiguous. European political risks (Italy referendum defeat, France fiscal dysfunction) are EUR-negative while Japan's political situation under Takaichi is stable. Golden Week thin liquidity (April 29-May 5) amplifies any BoJ-driven move. The net assessment is neutral because the Iran outcome's EUR/JPY direction is genuinely symmetric — any pathway can be constructed as either EUR/JPY-positive or negative depending on the specific channel that dominates.
key_bilateral_metrics: April 6: Iran deadline (binary; mildly EUR/JPY-bullish on escalation, bearish on de-escalation)
April 27-28: BoJ MPM + Outlook Report (bearish EUR/JPY if hike delivered)
April 30: ECB + Eurozone Q1 GDP (ambiguous)
April 29-May 5: Golden Week liquidity vacuum
Italy/France political risk: EUR-negative background
tactical.what_is_priced: The Iran deadline is not priced as a binary event in EUR/JPY — the cross has been flat. The BoJ hike is ~70% priced. The compound probability of multiple events occurring in the same week creates tail risk that is underpriced.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Over 1-6 months, the event calendar includes the G7 Summit (June 15-17, Evian), where FX policy discussions could address competitive devaluation concerns. Any communiqué language on yen weakness would be directly EUR/JPY-relevant. Japan's Takaichi will attend for the first time as PM. The Section 301 investigations targeting Japan (~July 2026 completion) could create bilateral trade disruption. The November US midterms introduce broad USD uncertainty. None of these events has a clear directional EUR/JPY signal — each could be constructed as positive or negative for the pair.
key_bilateral_metrics: G7 Summit June 15-17: potential FX policy discussion
Section 301 Japan completion: ~July 2026
US midterms: November 2026
BoJ meeting schedule: April, June, July (three potential catalysts)
strategic.what_is_priced: Strategic events are too distant to be priced. The G7 Summit's potential to generate FX-relevant communication about yen weakness is an underpriced catalyst.

factors.horizon_split: Both horizons neutral with low confidence. Events are genuinely symmetric for EUR/JPY.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: The bilateral fiscal picture presents an unusual configuration: both the eurozone and Japan have fiscal concerns, but of fundamentally different character. Japan's gross debt/GDP at ~236% is the highest in the developed world, with S&P's March 30 warning explicitly linking yen weakness to potential downgrade risk. The eurozone faces peripheral stress (BTP-Bund at 95bp, OAT-Bund at 70-75bp) and France's fiscal dysfunction. Tactically, neither fiscal situation is generating a directional EUR/JPY signal. The S&P warning on Japan is JPY-negative (bearish EUR/JPY would mean the opposite — the warning is EUR/JPY-supportive by implication). But the eurozone's peripheral spread widening constrains ECB hiking, which is EUR-negative. The two fiscal concerns roughly cancel each other.
key_bilateral_metrics: Japan gross debt/GDP: ~236% vs eurozone average: ~90%
S&P Japan: A+ stable (warning on yen link to downgrade)
BTP-Bund: 95bp (rising); OAT-Bund: 70-75bp
Japan FY2026 budget: JPY122T (record) vs Germany fiscal expansion: €500bn
10 EU countries under Excessive Deficit Procedures
tactical.what_is_priced: Fiscal risk is a structural non-event for EUR/JPY. Neither fiscal situation is acute enough to generate near-term FX moves.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Strategically, the German fiscal expansion is the eurozone's strongest asset — and it has no Japanese equivalent in terms of structural transformation. The €500bn infrastructure fund addresses Europe's chronic growth underperformance narrative. Japan's fiscal expansion under Takaichi is reflationary but compounds an already-deteriorating debt trajectory. Over 6 months, the German fiscal impulse begins to flow into GDP data, providing a structural EUR-positive factor that Japan cannot match. This is the only factor where the strategic view is bullish EUR/JPY.
key_bilateral_metrics: German fiscal boost: +0.3-0.5pp GDP by 2027
Japan fiscal trajectory: deteriorating (IMF: interest bill to double 2025-2031)
JGB market structure shift: BoJ tapering + declining liquidity in super-longs
EU defence spending ramp: €800bn ReArm Europe programme
strategic.what_is_priced: The German fiscal expansion is partially priced in EUR/USD and Bund yields but not yet in EUR/JPY. If the growth impulse materialises, EUR/JPY has room to rerate higher on this factor alone — but it is a slow-burn story.

factors.horizon_split: Tactically neutral as neither fiscal situation creates near-term directional pressure. Strategically mildly bullish EUR/JPY as the German fiscal expansion advantage builds.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: EUR/JPY's market structure shows a pair that has stalled near its all-time high (186.88) and is compressing in a narrowing range. Spot at 183.63 sits barely above the 50-day SMA (183.20) and the 100-day SMA (183.64) — a flat configuration that could resolve in either direction. The 200-day SMA at 180.34 is rising and provides structural support. ActionForex identifies 181.27 as the critical 38.2% retracement level that must hold for the bullish structure to remain intact. Babypips notes the pair is 'compressing below a clear supply zone, and repeated failure near 184.8–186.0 keeps the risk of a downside rotation alive,' with a confirmed break below 182.0 targeting 179.5. The bearish technical case rests on the failed breakout above 186 creating a potential double-top structure, RSI divergence noted by multiple sources, and the MACD bearish crossover on the daily timeframe. The BoJ meeting creates an asymmetric vol event: a hike would produce a 200-400 pip EUR/JPY decline while a hold would produce only a 100-150 pip rally, given the 70% pricing.
key_bilateral_metrics: Spot vs 50d SMA: 183.63 vs 183.20 (marginally above)
Spot vs 100d SMA: 183.63 vs 183.64 (flat)
Spot vs 200d SMA: 183.63 vs 180.34 (above by 329 pips)
Support: 182.00-182.80 (demand zone), 180.78, 181.27 (38.2% retracement)
Resistance: 184.75, 186.00-186.88 (all-time high)
tactical.what_is_priced: The near-term range (182-186) is well-defined by the technical community. What is underpriced is the asymmetric vol event from the BoJ meeting — a hike delivers a larger move than a hold because it triggers positioning adjustments across the entire JPY complex. Options on EUR/JPY are likely cheap relative to the event density.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Over 1-6 months, the pair's structural trajectory depends on whether the 186.88 all-time high is breached or the correction deepens. ActionForex's bigger-picture view suggests that a firm break of 186.86 would pave the way to a 78.6% projection target at 194.88. Conversely, a break below 180.78 would confirm a trend reversal. The vol regime should normalise over 6 months as geopolitical event risk fades, but in the interim the cluster of BoJ meetings (April, June, July) creates recurring repricing events that benefit EUR/JPY bears through the staircase effect — each BoJ hike produces a step lower.
key_bilateral_metrics: Bullish breakout target: 189.81 (61.8% projection), then 194.88 (78.6%)
Bearish breakdown target: 180.78 (correction low), then 175 (medium-term channel)
BoJ meeting staircase: April, June, July — three potential step-down events
Daily turnover: EUR/JPY is a liquid cross (~$100bn+/day)
strategic.what_is_priced: Exchange rate aggregators project EUR/JPY declining from 185.24 in early 2026 to 182.27 by late 2026 and 175.07 by early 2027 — a gradual decline consistent with BoJ normalisation. The risk is that the decline is faster and sharper if catalysts cluster.

factors.horizon_split: Tactically bearish (60) on the compressed range resolving lower via the BoJ catalyst. Strategically neutral (45) as the structural trend could persist if the BoJ disappoints or the pair breaks to new highs.

expression_assessment.vol_landscape_summary: EUR/JPY implied vol is estimated at 10-12% (1M), in a low-to-moderate regime. The pair is compressing in a narrowing range near the all-time high, creating a coiled spring structure. Event density in late April (BoJ April 27-28 + ECB April 30 + Golden Week) creates a binary repricing environment that the current vol level likely underestimates.
expression_assessment.directional_expression: The directional bearish view (lower EUR/JPY) is best expressed through a combination of spot short and long EUR/JPY put options. Spot short offers clean carry (only -60 pips over 6M) with a defined stop above 186.88 (all-time high). Put options with strikes around 182-183 and expiry just after the BoJ April 27-28 meeting are structurally cheap relative to the asymmetric payoff — a 25bp hike produces 200-400 pips of downside versus only 100-150 pips of upside on a hold. The combination of delta-1 short spot and long puts provides convexity for the tail risk of a 50bp BoJ hike.
expression_assessment.event_timing_consideration: The BoJ April 27-28 meeting is the key expression timing anchor. Options expiring April 29-30 (just after the BoJ statement but capturing ECB April 30) are optimal for tactical expression. Golden Week (April 29-May 5) creates a liquidity vacuum that can amplify any BoJ-driven move by 100-200 pips — theta decay through Golden Week is a risk for long option holders but the vol event likely compensates. For spot trades, sizing should allow for a 100-150 pip adverse move against position if the BoJ holds.
expression_assessment.liquidity_note: EUR/JPY is a highly liquid cross with estimated daily turnover of $100bn+, ranking among the top 5 G10 crosses. Bid-offer spreads are typically 1-3 pips in liquid hours. Liquidity degrades materially during Golden Week (April 29-May 5) when Japanese markets are closed — execution should be completed or size reduced before April 28 close. Option market-making in EUR/JPY is active, ensuring reasonable execution for vanilla puts in the 182-185 strike range.

factors.weight_adjustment_reason: Non-USD cross; USD channel is indirect and muted. Weight reduced -0.03 on both horizons.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: For EUR/JPY as a cross pair, USD funding conditions affect both legs through the vehicle currency channel — most FX trades settle in USD. SOFR at 3.63% is stable with no acute stress. The BoJ USD swap line shows only $30.3M outstanding (essentially zero, no stress). ECB-Fed swap line shows no significant draws. Both EUR and JPY are funded through USD, and both face similar USD funding conditions. The cross-currency basis for EUR/JPY specifically is not available, but the absence of stress on either USD swap line suggests no acute basis dislocation. The dollar smile positions USD on the left leg (risk-off strong), which is generically bearish for both EUR and JPY — but since both are affected similarly, the net EUR/JPY impact is minimal. Quarter-end effects (March 31) may create temporary distortion but resolve within days.
key_bilateral_metrics: SOFR: 3.63% (orderly)
BoJ USD swap line: $30.3M outstanding (no stress)
ECB-Fed swap line: no significant draws
Dollar smile: left leg (risk-off) — affects both EUR and JPY equally
tactical.what_is_priced: USD funding is orderly and priced as such. No bilateral mispricing exists for EUR/JPY through the USD channel.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Over 1-6 months, the USD funding picture tilts toward EUR/JPY-bearish through an indirect channel. If the dollar smile transitions from left leg (risk-off strong USD) to the goldilocks middle (weak USD on stable growth), this benefits JPY more than EUR because JPY is more deeply undervalued (BIS REER at all-time low) and the BoJ's normalisation provides a structural catalyst for JPY revaluation that EUR lacks. The Fed's potential rate cut in H2 2026 would narrow the UST-JGB spread more than the UST-Bund spread (because JGB yields are rising while Bund yields are already elevated), creating a USD-JPY compression dynamic that feeds into EUR/JPY through the cross. Rising JGB yields (10Y at 2.36%) make domestic bonds more attractive for Japanese institutions, potentially reducing overseas allocation and USD/EUR borrowing needs — a structural JPY-positive, EUR/JPY-negative flow.
key_bilateral_metrics: Dollar smile transition: benefits JPY more than EUR (deeper undervaluation)
Fed cut scenario: narrows UST-JGB spread more than UST-Bund
Rising JGB yields: 10Y at 2.36% (attracting domestic capital home)
Powell succession (May 15): USD uncertainty — mildly EUR/JPY-bearish if USD weakens more vs JPY
strategic.what_is_priced: The structural forces favouring JPY over EUR in the USD liquidity channel are slow-burning and not priced at the strategic horizon.

factors.horizon_split: Tactically neutral (50) because USD funding is orderly and affects both legs equally. Strategically bearish EUR/JPY (50) because the dollar smile transition and JGB yield dynamics disproportionately benefit JPY.

adjustments.reason: EUR/JPY is a moderate-to-high risk-sensitivity cross; risk-off has outsized pair impact. Weight increased +0.03 on both tactical and strategic horizons.
Non-USD cross; USD channel is indirect and muted. Weight reduced -0.03 on both tactical and strategic horizons.

tactical.score_interpretation: The net weighted score of -0.504 is firmly in bearish territory. Six of ten factors are bearish for EUR/JPY, four are neutral, and none are bullish. The score maps to conviction 3/5, reflecting a clear directional signal tempered by timing uncertainty around the Iran conflict.
tactical.rationale: The tactical bearish EUR/JPY case is dominated by the rate differential narrowing dynamic (Factor 1: -0.168, the single largest contributor) combined with the bilateral inflation-growth divergence (Factor 2: -0.117) — together these two factors account for 57% of the total negative score. The BoJ is poised to deliver a 25bp hike to 1.00% on April 27-28 with ~70% probability, while the ECB is likely to hold on April 30 without new staff projections — creating a clear policy divergence event within the tactical horizon. The risk appetite channel (Factor 3: -0.078, boosted to 0.13 weight for this risk-sensitive cross) reinforces the bearish case as equities remain under pressure, with the VIX at ~30 and the Nikkei 225 down 11.5% from highs. Positioning (Factor 4: -0.065) provides the amplification mechanism: the massive EUR AM long (+288K contracts, 95th percentile) combined with moderate JPY shorts (-63K) creates a bilateral unwind catalyst — if both positions reverse simultaneously, the EUR/JPY move is materially larger than either individual currency's move versus USD. Guidance (Factor 6: -0.046) reinforces the rate differential factor: the BoJ's 8-1 hold with an explicit April hike signal contrasts sharply with the ECB's split Governing Council and no new projections at the April 30 meeting. Market structure (Factor 9: -0.030) shows the pair compressing below a supply zone with RSI divergence and MACD bearish crossover — technically coiled for a downside break. FXStreet's Q2 outlook explicitly identifies EUR/JPY as one of the strongest relative-value expressions, citing 'ECB growth fragility versus BoJ tightening bias.' The path of least resistance is lower toward the 182-183 area over the next 2-4 weeks, with the April 27-28 BoJ meeting as the primary repricing event. The key risk to the bearish thesis is an ECB surprise hike on April 30 or an Iran de-escalation that collapses oil and supports risk-on (EUR/JPY-positive). Conviction is 3/5 because the direction is clear but timing uncertainty around the Iran conflict and the possibility that both central banks surprise in the same direction limits position sizing confidence.
tactical.g10_relative_ranking: EUR/JPY ranks as one of the top 2-3 JPY shorts to fade (i.e., short EUR/JPY is among the most attractive JPY longs). Among EUR crosses, short EUR/JPY is more attractive than short EUR/CHF (CHF lacks a rate catalyst) but less clean than short EUR/NOK (NOK benefits directly from oil). Among JPY pairs, short EUR/JPY is preferred over short USD/JPY (avoids dollar smile headwind) and comparable to short AUD/JPY (which has the larger risk-off beta but higher carry cost).

conditions: BoJ hikes 25bp to 1.00% at April 27-28 meeting
Iran conflict persists at current intensity (no escalation or de-escalation)
EUR AM longs reduce by 50-80K contracts
base_case.path: EUR/JPY grinds lower toward the 182-183 demand zone through the first three weeks of April as the market positions for the BoJ meeting. The Iran deadline (April 6) introduces a brief volatility spike — escalation could temporarily push the pair higher by 100-150 pips on oil fears, but this creates a better entry for shorts. The BoJ meeting itself (April 27-28) is the discrete repricing event: a 25bp hike pushes EUR/JPY to ~180-181 in the initial 24-48 hours, with Golden Week thin liquidity potentially amplifying the move to 178-180.

conditions: ECB signals June hike or delivers surprise at April meeting while BoJ holds on extreme uncertainty
Risk-on reversal boosts carry trade re-engagement
BoJ hikes 50bp or signals accelerated normalisation pace
Simultaneous EUR AM liquidation (+50-100K reduction) + JPY short covering

tactical.path_expectation: EUR/JPY is most likely to grind lower toward the 182-183 demand zone through the first three weeks of April as the market positions for the BoJ meeting. The Iran deadline (April 6) introduces a brief volatility spike — escalation could temporarily push the pair higher (by 100-150 pips on oil fears), but this would create a better entry for EUR/JPY shorts as the BoJ meeting approaches. The BoJ meeting itself (April 27-28) is the discrete repricing event: a 25bp hike should push EUR/JPY to ~180-181 in the initial 24-48 hours, with Golden Week thin liquidity potentially amplifying the move to 178-180. The ECB meeting on April 30 (expected hold) provides no offsetting catalyst in the base case. Shape: sideways with a downward tilt into the BoJ meeting, followed by a sharp step-down on delivery, with potential for 100-200 pip overshoot during Golden Week.

increase_conditions: BoJ delivers 25bp hike + hawkish forward guidance signalling July follow-up AND EUR/JPY breaks below 182.00 on daily close
BoJ delivers 50bp surprise AND EUR AM positioning drops below +200K contracts AND EUR/JPY breaks below 180.00
decrease_conditions: BoJ holds with dovish statement removing tightening bias language AND Brent crude falls below $90/bbl
ECB hikes 25bp at April 30 meeting while BoJ holds AND EUR/JPY reclaims 185.00

risk_reward.risk_reward_ratio: Weighted payoff approximately 2.5:1 in favour of the bearish thesis
risk_reward.asymmetry_assessment: Moderately positive asymmetry. The downside scenario for the pair (30% probability, 563 pips) delivers larger absolute gains than the upside risk (237 pips), and the base case is positive. The BoJ hike creates a one-directional catalyst that options markets are underpricing.

strategic.score_interpretation: The net weighted score of -0.220 is weakly to moderately bearish, mapping to conviction 3/5 based on the qualitative assessment of the normalisation trajectory exceeding what the mechanical score implies. Two factors bear and one is bullish, with the remaining seven neutral.
strategic.rationale: The strategic bearish EUR/JPY thesis rests on the BoJ's multi-step normalisation cycle delivering 1-2 additional hikes to 1.00-1.25% over the coming quarters, progressively compressing the ECB-BoJ rate differential from +125bp toward +50-100bp. Factor 1 (Policy & Rates) dominates at -0.144, reflecting the single most important bilateral driver: the BoJ is moving toward tighter policy with greater conviction than the ECB. Factor 6 (Guidance & Credibility) contributes -0.066 as the BoJ's communication trajectory remains more hawkish and credible than the ECB's ambiguous signals across the April-July meeting sequence. Factor 10 (USD Liquidity) adds -0.045 as the dollar smile transition and rising JGB yields (10Y at 2.36%) disproportionately benefit JPY relative to EUR. The sole bullish offset comes from Factor 8 (Fiscal & Sovereign) at +0.035, where the German fiscal expansion (€500bn infrastructure fund) provides a structural EUR advantage that Japan cannot match — but this is a slow-burn catalyst. The aggregate bank consensus for EUR/JPY shows wide dispersion: aggregated research projections point to EUR/JPY declining from 185 in early 2026 to approximately 182 by late 2026 and 175 by early 2027, while LongForecast projects EUR/JPY ending March at 187 and rising to 193-197 by August-September 2026, and PandaForecast's weighted average targets decline from 178.58 in April 2026 to 174.01 by July 2026. The consensus is directionally aligned with our view (lower EUR/JPY) but there is significant disagreement on magnitude. The German fiscal expansion and potential ECB hiking cycle are the primary risks to the bearish thesis — if the eurozone successfully navigates the energy shock and the ECB hikes aggressively, the carry could revert in EUR's favour. But this requires the energy shock to be both inflationary enough to justify ECB hikes and transitory enough to allow growth recovery — a narrow path that our base case does not assign majority probability.
strategic.g10_relative_ranking: EUR/JPY ranks as the 2nd most attractive JPY long (pair short) behind USD/JPY among G10 crosses. The rationale: EUR faces more headwinds than USD (energy shock, stagflation, positioning overhang) while sharing the same JPY normalisation catalyst. Among EUR crosses, short EUR/JPY is attractive because JPY benefits from the BoJ's unique normalisation cycle — no other EUR cross offers a comparable policy divergence catalyst on the quote currency side.

conditions: Oil settles to $85-95 range as Iran situation stabilises
German fiscal spending begins to flow but not yet GDP-visible
base_case.path: Staircase decline: each BoJ meeting (April, June, July) delivers a discrete repricing step of 200-300 pips lower. Phase 1 (April-May): BoJ hike to 1.00% triggers move from 183 to 179-181, amplified by Golden Week. Phase 2 (June): consolidation at 178-182 as market awaits ECB June meeting and BoJ QT assessment; ECB hike partially offsets. Phase 3 (July-September): BoJ July Outlook Report + potential second hike pushes EUR/JPY toward 175-178.

conditions: Iran resolved quickly, ECB hikes 2-3 times while BoJ pauses after 1.00%
German fiscal stimulus accelerates and shows in GDP data; carry trade re-engages on widening differential
BoJ hikes aggressively to 1.25%+ with July follow-through
EUR AM positioning liquidates fully and eurozone enters recession
Carry trade unwind accelerates with JPY short covering cascade

strategic.path_expectation: The strategic path is a staircase decline — each BoJ meeting (April, June, July) delivers a discrete repricing event that steps EUR/JPY lower by 200-300 pips. Phase 1 (April-May): BoJ hike to 1.00% triggers the move from 183 to 179-181; Golden Week amplifies the move through thin liquidity. Phase 2 (June): consolidation at 178-182 as the market awaits the ECB June meeting with staff projections and the BoJ QT interim assessment; an ECB hike partially offsets the BoJ-driven decline but the net direction remains lower. Phase 3 (July-September): BoJ July Outlook Report with updated FY2027 forecasts and a potential second hike to 1.25% pushes EUR/JPY toward the 175-178 area. The pattern is step-down on BoJ events with 150-250 pip countertrend rallies between them, driven by carry re-accumulation and risk-on episodes in between the catalyst events.

increase_conditions: BoJ reaches 1.00% AND delivers hawkish July guidance signalling further hikes AND Brent crude falls below $95
BoJ reaches 1.25% AND carry trade unwind triggers AUD/JPY below 105 AND EUR/JPY breaks below 175
decrease_conditions: ECB hikes 50bp+ AND BoJ pauses at 1.00% for extended period
ECB delivers 3 hikes AND German GDP accelerates above 1.5% annualised AND BoJ reverses normalisation guidance

risk_reward.risk_reward_ratio: Weighted payoff approximately 3:1 in favour of the bearish thesis
risk_reward.asymmetry_assessment: Positive asymmetry. The downside scenario (30% probability, 1063 pips) is materially larger than the upside risk (537 pips), and the base case is firmly positive. The staircase BoJ normalisation provides recurring catalysts that options markets can underprice at each step.

consensus_divergence.bank_consensus_forecast: Exchange Rates UK aggregated bank research forecasts EUR/JPY declining from 185.24 in early 2026 to 182.27 by September 2026. LongForecast projects EUR/JPY rising to 187 in March and then to 193-197 by August-September 2026. PandaForecast targets a decline to 174-179 over the next 3-6 months.
consensus_divergence.our_tactical_view: Bearish EUR/JPY; target 180-182 over 2-4 weeks; conviction 3/5
consensus_divergence.our_strategic_view: Bearish EUR/JPY; target 175-179 by September 2026; conviction 3/5
consensus_divergence.divergence_direction: Below consensus on strategic horizon (more bearish than Exchange Rates UK average; roughly in line with PandaForecast; sharply below LongForecast)
consensus_divergence.divergence_magnitude: Approximately 300-400 pips more bearish than the Exchange Rates UK consensus of ~182 by September 2026
consensus_divergence.why_we_differ: We assign higher weight to the BoJ's normalisation pace and the bilateral positioning unwind dynamic than the consensus does. FXStreet notes that 'the Bank of Japan still carries the strongest tightening bias in G10' while 'the ECB and BoE remain on hold, but renewed inflation pressure does not fix weak growth.' We share this assessment and translate it into a sharper bearish EUR/JPY view than the consensus average of ~182-185. Specifically, the consensus underestimates: (1) the probability of 2 BoJ hikes by July vs the 1 hike implicitly embedded in consensus; (2) the bilateral positioning unwind dynamic — the EUR AM long at 95th percentile combined with moderate JPY shorts creates a compressed move potential of 300-500 pips that a gradual decline scenario ignores; and (3) the staircase effect of recurring BoJ catalysts creating recurring step-downs across April, June, and July meetings.
consensus_divergence.payoff_if_right: 400-800 pips of EUR/JPY decline from current levels; +3.6% to +7.2% on a spot short position
consensus_divergence.risk_if_wrong: The consensus bullish models (LongForecast, etc.) prove correct and EUR/JPY rallies to 190+, representing a 600-700 pip loss from entry

cross_horizon_consistency.explanation: The tactical and strategic views are aligned directionally (both bearish EUR/JPY) with identical conviction scores. Tactical conviction is supported by the specific April BoJ catalyst while strategic conviction is supported by the multi-step normalisation trajectory through June and July meetings. The tactical thesis transitions naturally into the strategic thesis: if the BoJ delivers in April, the same dynamic extends through June and July meetings. If the BoJ disappoints in April, both horizons weaken simultaneously.
cross_horizon_consistency.trading_implication: Establish the short EUR/JPY position for the tactical horizon; if the BoJ delivers in April, extend the position through the strategic horizon targeting 175-179. The staircase decline structure means each BoJ meeting provides an opportunity to add or maintain the position, with 150-250 pip countertrend rallies between meetings offering better entry points.

catalysts.event: Tankan Q1 2026 Release + New Fiscal Year
catalysts.rationale: A strong Tankan reading confirms Japan's economic resilience and removes barriers to the April BoJ hike. The new fiscal year triggers fresh institutional allocation decisions by Japanese asset managers and pension funds, which can produce significant FX flows. Strong business confidence is JPY-supportive through the BoJ rate expectations channel, as it validates the wage-price mechanism and provides the BoJ with the data cover needed to deliver the April hike.

A hot print (2.5-3.0%+) validates the ECB's hawkish pivot and could raise April hike odds — EUR/JPY-bullish via carry widening. But the stagflation narrative (high inflation + weak growth) is EUR-negative more broadly. The net pair effect depends on which channel dominates: if the market reads the hot print as ECB-hawkish, EUR/JPY rallies; if it reads it as confirming the growth-damaging stagflation scenario, EUR/JPY falls. National flash CPI prints from Germany, France, and Spain have already telegraphed the direction of the eurozone-wide release.

catalysts.event: Trump Iran Strike Pause Expiry / Iran Deadline
catalysts.rationale: The April 6 Iran deadline is the dominant binary event for the pair. Escalation pushes oil higher — marginally more JPY-negative than EUR-negative given Japan's 90% energy import dependency versus eurozone's 58%, so mildly EUR/JPY-bullish on escalation. De-escalation collapses oil and restores JPY safe-haven function through the energy cost relief channel — sharply EUR/JPY-bearish because JPY's terms-of-trade improvement exceeds EUR's. The asymmetric payoff structure of the two outcomes means de-escalation is a larger EUR/JPY move than escalation, making the expected value of the event mildly EUR/JPY-bearish.

catalysts.event: BoJ Monetary Policy Meeting + Outlook Report
catalysts.rationale: THE critical bilateral catalyst. A 25bp hike to 1.00% with ~70% OIS probability would narrow the ECB-BoJ differential to +100bp and trigger broad JPY short covering across all crosses, including EUR/JPY. The updated Outlook Report will likely revise CPI forecasts higher on the energy shock, providing the BoJ with the justification for continued normalisation. Even a hold with hawkish tone would support JPY modestly through the forward guidance channel. A 50bp surprise is near-zero priced but would be explosively bearish for EUR/JPY — the BoJ's Summary of Opinions shows this is being actively debated internally. Golden Week immediately follows, creating thin liquidity that could amplify any move.

catalysts.event: ECB Rate Decision + Eurozone Q1 GDP Flash
catalysts.rationale: No new staff projections makes a hike unlikely at the April 30 ECB meeting. A hold with hawkish language is the base case. Q1 GDP likely near-flat (0.0-0.1% QoQ) — weak but not surprising given the Iran energy shock. If the ECB surprises with a hike, it would be EUR/JPY-bullish through carry widening, potentially offsetting the BoJ's move from April 27-28. The proximity to the BoJ meeting (same week) creates a compound event risk: if both central banks surprise in opposite directions simultaneously, EUR/JPY volatility spikes sharply.

Japanese market holidays create a liquidity vacuum that amplifies any BoJ-driven move from April 27-28. If the BoJ hikes and markets gap EUR/JPY lower, thin Golden Week liquidity could produce an additional 100-200 pip overshoot beyond the fundamental repricing. Dealers reduce risk appetite and widen bid-offer spreads during this period. The liquidity effect is a structural amplifier rather than a directional signal — it makes the BoJ move larger in both directions.

catalysts.event: ECB Rate Decision + Staff Economic Projections
catalysts.rationale: The key strategic ECB meeting includes updated staff projections — the first with full data incorporating the energy shock and Germany's fiscal expansion. Updated projections showing persistent above-target inflation with moderate growth would make a rate hike highly likely, widening the ECB-BoJ carry differential and providing EUR/JPY a structural tailwind. This is the most probable date for the first ECB hike since 2022. An ECB hike at this meeting would partially offset the BoJ-driven decline in EUR/JPY from April and represent the primary upside risk to the bearish strategic thesis.

catalysts.event: G7 Summit, Evian + BoJ MPM + QT Interim Assessment
catalysts.rationale: Two significant events coincide in mid-June. The G7 Summit (June 15-17, Evian) could generate FX policy communiqué language addressing yen weakness — Japan's Takaichi attending for the first time as PM makes this particularly relevant; any coordinated statement on competitive devaluation would be directly EUR/JPY-relevant as it would cap JPY strength. Simultaneously, the BoJ June MPM conducts its interim assessment of JGB purchase reduction, with an acceleration of tapering being JPY-positive and EUR/JPY-bearish. The two events could work in opposite directions.

catalysts.event: BoJ MPM + Outlook Report (Potential Second Hike)
catalysts.rationale: The July BoJ meeting with updated Outlook Report is historically one of the most consequential for FX markets, as it includes FY2027 forecasts and a comprehensive assessment of the normalisation path. A second hike to 1.25% would further compress the ECB-BoJ differential toward +75-100bp (depending on ECB actions in June). Even without a hike, hawkish forward guidance from the updated Outlook Report would reprice the JPY complex. This is the final step in the strategic thesis staircase — if both the April and July hikes are delivered, EUR/JPY is on trajectory toward 175 or below.

The appointment of a new Fed Chair with potentially different policy preferences introduces USD uncertainty that affects EUR/JPY through the vehicle currency channel. A more dovish Fed Chair would accelerate rate cut expectations, weakening USD broadly — this would narrow the UST-JGB spread faster than the UST-Bund spread (because JGB yields are rising toward parity), disproportionately benefiting JPY over EUR and thus being EUR/JPY-bearish. A hawkish appointment would maintain USD strength, which is generically EUR/JPY-neutral as both legs suffer equally versus the dollar.

invalidators.condition: BoJ holds at April 27-28 meeting with dovish statement explicitly removing tightening bias language
invalidators.new_stance: Neutral EUR/JPY — both tactical and strategic bearish theses significantly delayed
invalidators.mechanism: The primary tactical catalyst is removed; rate differential thesis delayed indefinitely. The market would unwind the 70% priced April hike, causing JPY to weaken across the board. EUR/JPY could rally 150-250 pips as carry trade re-engages and JPY shorts rebuild.
invalidators.monitor_indicator: BoJ April 27-28 policy statement and press conference language

invalidators.condition: EUR/JPY breaks above 186.88 all-time high on a daily close basis
invalidators.new_stance: Bullish EUR/JPY — technical structure confirms trend resumption
invalidators.mechanism: A daily close above the all-time high at 186.88 invalidates the double-top technical pattern and confirms trend resumption toward 189.81 (61.8% projection) and 194.88 (78.6% projection). This would trigger stop-loss orders above the ATH and momentum algos, producing a cascading rally that invalidates the range-bound bearish setup.

invalidators.condition: ECB delivers a 50bp rate hike at a single meeting while BoJ holds
invalidators.new_stance: Bullish EUR/JPY — rate differential widens decisively in EUR's favour
invalidators.mechanism: A 50bp ECB hike would widen the rate differential from +125bp to +175bp, removing the carry compression narrative entirely. Combined with a BoJ hold, the bilateral policy divergence reverses sharply — the primary driver of the bearish thesis flips to bullish. EUR/JPY would rally 300-400 pips on the day.

invalidators.condition: Brent crude falls below $80/bbl sustained for 2+ weeks
invalidators.new_stance: More bearish EUR/JPY — accelerates the bearish thesis through ToT channel
invalidators.mechanism: A sustained fall below $80 (from ~$111-113) would produce asymmetric benefits for JPY over EUR. Japan's 90% energy import dependency means its terms-of-trade improvement would be proportionally larger than the eurozone's, strengthening JPY more than EUR. This would reinforce the BoJ normalisation channel and add a ToT-driven JPY appreciation dynamic to the rate differential channel, creating a dual-catalyst bearish EUR/JPY environment.

invalidators.condition: CFTC EUR asset manager net long falls below +150K contracts AND JPY net short positions exceed -120K contracts (rebuilding)
invalidators.new_stance: Neutral EUR/JPY — bilateral positioning dynamic has reversed; no more unwind fuel
invalidators.mechanism: If the EUR AM long liquidates to +150K (from +288K), the positioning overhang that amplifies the bearish thesis has been removed. If simultaneously JPY shorts rebuild to -120K+, the JPY carry trade is re-engaging rather than unwinding. Both legs of the bilateral positioning thesis would have normalised, removing the amplification mechanism from the bearish case.
invalidators.monitor_indicator: CFTC Commitments of Traders weekly report — EUR non-commercial and JPY non-commercial positions

signposts.confirms_if: Narrows below 50bp (BoJ normalisation compressing the rate differential faster than Bund yields can keep pace)
signposts.invalidates_if: Widens above 100bp (ECB hiking faster than BoJ, reversing the carry compression narrative)
signposts.bilateral_importance: The Bund-JGB 10Y spread is the most important real-time indicator of ECB-BoJ rate convergence, which is the primary driver of EUR/JPY. Changes in this spread directly precede EUR/JPY repricing events — narrowing signals JPY strength, widening signals EUR strength. This indicator is unique to the EUR/JPY pair and has no equivalent relevance for other crosses.

signposts.confirms_if: Spot approaches and tests the 200d SMA (decline toward 180-181), signalling the correction has broken through intermediate support
signposts.invalidates_if: Spot breaks above 186.00 decisively on a daily close basis, confirming trend resumption and invalidating the double-top structure
signposts.bilateral_importance: The 200d SMA at 180.34 is the key structural support level for EUR/JPY — a test would confirm that the BoJ-driven repricing is producing a meaningful trend reversal rather than a temporary correction. The 200d SMA is a universal institutional reference that triggers systematic rebalancing flows when approached.

signposts.indicator: BoJ OIS-Implied Rate Path (April and cumulative)
signposts.current_level: ~70% probability of April hike; ~166% cumulative by October
signposts.confirms_if: April probability rises above 80% AND cumulative 12-month pricing exceeds 200% (implying 2+ hikes firmly expected)
signposts.invalidates_if: April probability drops below 50%, signalling the market no longer views April as a live meeting
signposts.bilateral_importance: The BoJ rate path is the single most important driver of EUR/JPY — shifts in OIS pricing directly move the pair through the rate differential channel. This indicator is uniquely important for EUR/JPY versus other EUR crosses because JPY's carry dynamics are primarily BoJ-driven rather than being influenced by commodity prices, growth differentials, or other factors that dominate in EUR/AUD, EUR/NOK, etc.

signposts.indicator: CFTC EUR Asset Manager Net Long Position
signposts.confirms_if: Declines 50K or more in a single weekly report (signalling liquidation has begun and the amplification mechanism is activating)
signposts.invalidates_if: Increases above +300K contracts (bulls are adding conviction at the ATH zone, invalidating the positioning unwind thesis)
signposts.bilateral_importance: The EUR AM long overhang is the primary amplification mechanism for EUR/JPY downside — if it liquidates simultaneously with JPY short covering, the cross move is significantly larger than either individual currency's move versus USD. This positioning signal is specific to EUR/JPY; the same EUR AM long does not affect EUR/CHF or EUR/GBP in the same way because those crosses lack the bilateral JPY short covering dynamic.
signposts.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders weekly report

signposts.confirms_if: Falls below $90/bbl sustained — JPY benefits more than EUR from energy cost relief given 90% vs 58% import dependency, reinforcing JPY strength through the ToT channel
signposts.invalidates_if: Rises above $130/bbl sustained — overwhelms the BoJ normalisation thesis through catastrophic ToT destruction, potentially forcing BoJ to pause normalisation
signposts.bilateral_importance: The energy shock affects both EUR and JPY but in different magnitudes and through different channels (TTF gas for EUR, Brent/JKM for JPY). The direction of oil resolution determines whether JPY or EUR benefits more from any reversal — making it a key bilateral discriminator. Oil's direction is particularly important for EUR/JPY because it simultaneously affects the terms-of-trade channel (where Japan is more exposed) and the inflation channel (where the eurozone stagflation dynamic is more severe).

signposts.confirms_if: Falls below 105 — confirms JPY is strengthening across the board, validating the carry unwind thesis that drives EUR/JPY lower through the cross-currency JPY appreciation dynamic
signposts.invalidates_if: Rallies above 114 — confirms carry trade re-engagement and risk-on environment, making EUR/JPY shorts vulnerable to a sympathy rally
signposts.bilateral_importance: AUD/JPY is the purest JPY carry trade proxy in G10 FX due to AUD's high yield and commodity sensitivity combined with JPY's low yield and safe-haven status. Its direction confirms or invalidates the carry unwind thesis that underpins EUR/JPY's bearish case — if AUD/JPY is weakening, EUR/JPY will follow through the JPY-strengthening channel, whereas if AUD/JPY is rallying, the carry unwind thesis has reversed and EUR/JPY shorts are at risk.

assumptions: EUR/JPY forward points are estimated from the +125bp policy rate differential using covered interest rate parity. Actual market forwards may differ due to cross-currency basis and liquidity factors — exact EUR/JPY forward points were not available from web searches and are behind terminal paywalls.

EUR/JPY implied volatility is inferred from component pair IVs (EUR/USD ~10-13%, USD/JPY ~9.76%) with correlation adjustment. Exact EUR/JPY IV and risk reversal data was not available from web searches and would require options terminal access.
The bilateral carry analysis uses policy rates (ECB 2.00% vs BoJ 0.75%) as the carry proxy. Actual overnight swap rates and cross-currency basis may differ from this simplified calculation.

Weight adjustments: Risk Appetite (Factor 3) boosted by +0.03 on both horizons because EUR/JPY's moderate risk-sensitivity means the current risk-off environment has outsized pair impact. USD Liquidity (Factor 10) reduced by -0.03 on both horizons because this is a non-USD cross where the vehicle currency channel is indirect and less material.

PPP estimates for EUR/JPY combine EUR dossier (~175 implied fair value) and JPY dossier (~104 implied bilateral fair value) — the wide discrepancy reflects different source methodologies and base years. The pair is materially above any PPP estimate, confirming structural JPY undervaluation as the dominant valuation theme.

Scenario probabilities (50/20/30 for both tactical and strategic horizons) are subjective assessments. The 30% downside scenario probability reflects the BoJ's asymmetric delivery probability and the bilateral positioning unwind potential, which could produce a move that is larger and faster than the base case gradual decline.

The EUR asset manager CFTC data (+287,940 contracts) is used as a proxy for EUR/JPY long positioning. In reality, these EUR AM positions may be expressed versus USD, GBP, or other currencies rather than JPY specifically. The bilateral pairing assumption may overstate the EUR/JPY positioning unwind risk.

The S&P Japan sovereign warning (March 30) was referenced but the specific warning language on yen weakness and downgrade risk was not directly confirmed — it is attributed to press reporting from the analysis period.

VIX level (~30) and Nikkei 225 decline (-11.5%) represent approximate levels at the time of writing; markets are dynamic and these reference points may have moved materially by the time of trade execution.

The LongForecast bullish projection (EUR/JPY rising to 193-197 by August-September 2026) represents an outlier forecast that relies on assumptions about ECB hiking aggressively and Iran resolution — included for completeness to illustrate consensus dispersion but not assigned meaningful probability in our framework.

complete_citations.title: Q2 2026 Forex Outlook: USD, EUR, JPY and Top FX Trades to Watch
Japan's Policy Normalisation and Yen Carry Trade
